cybersecurity_goal: Preserve the integrity, plausibility, authenticity, and freshness of safety-relevant BMS measurements and derived battery state.
Prevent unauthorized, stale, or state-inappropriate execution of contactor, precharge, charge or discharge, power-limit, and cooling commands.
Restrict diagnostic services, programming, routines, writes, resets, and fault clearing to authorized identities, states, and ordered procedures.
Ensure BMS software is authentic, intact, compatible, non-downgraded, and recoverable before activation.
Prevent or detect unauthorized, incompatible, or internally inconsistent configuration and calibration changes.
Maintain timely critical communication and bounded, deterministic behavior during loss, malformed traffic, flooding, or processing stress.
Restrict BESS remote and site access to authenticated, authorized, and appropriately segmented identities, roles, functions, and zones.
Preserve trustworthy security events and engineering evidence and permit only controlled, authorized recovery and finding closure.
Restrict debug, bootloader, and console interfaces to authorized, authenticated, and lifecycle-appropriate use.
